 {"id":"frankenredis-r3lh","title":"Expand Lua scripting conformance with closure, recursion, and loop edge cases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-01T21:09:36.973663906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T21:09:41.110435975Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T21:09:41.110184499Z","close_reason":"24 new Lua conformance cases: closures with shared upvalues, local recursive functions (factorial, fibonacci), generic for loops (ipairs/pairs), nested loops, break, step, gmatch, table.sort, pcall/xpcall, string.format, math, table.maxn, os.clock","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r48","title":"Fix panicking unwrap in CONFIG SET parameter lookup","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-19T04:08:48.974371759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T04:09:46.983794195Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T04:09:46.983002218Z","close_reason":"Fixed: replaced unwrap() with expect() with descriptive message at CONFIG SET parameter lookup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r71v","title":"[HIGH] Reality-check vs upstream Redis 7+: CLUSTER FORGET/COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS, ACL GETUSER selectors, XADD MINID/LIMIT, XSETID ENTRIESADDED","description":"Run a focused parity audit against vendored redis-server (7.2.4) for the Redis 7-era surface that hasn't been live-oracle exercised yet. Specifically:

(a) CLUSTER subcommands beyond MYID/SHARDS/SLOTS/INFO: FORGET, COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS, RESET, BUMPEPOCH, FLUSHSLOTS, ADDSLOTSRANGE/DELSLOTSRANGE, LINKS. Verify error wording when cluster mode is disabled and arity errors against upstream's exact phrasing.
(b) ACL GETUSER selectors field (Redis 7.0+ multi-selector support): the field is structurally present in our reply but the channel/key/command vectors per selector may not match upstream when the user has zero selectors vs N>0 selectors.
(c) XADD MINID and LIMIT options (Redis 7.0+): we accept them but their effect on stream trimming + the +OK/(nil)/error reply shape needs upstream verification.
(d) XSETID with ENTRIESADDED and MAXDELETEDID arguments (Redis 7.2+).

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: spawn vendored redis-server, send each combination via the existing live-oracle harness, surface every divergence as a follow-up [HIGH] bead. File one bead per family that fails.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["acl","cluster","reality-check","redis7","streams"]}
-{"id":"frankenredis-r72v","title":"[HIGH] RESP3 protocol parity: HELLO 3 push-frame routing + map/set/double/big-number types in real handlers","description":"Our parser learned RESP3 reply types via ParserConfig::allow_resp3 (br-frankenredis-ozcx), but the runtime handlers haven't been audited end-to-end for the RESP3-only reply shapes:

(a) HELLO 3: server response should include a Map instead of an Array for the metadata; verify client receives '%' framing not '*'.
(b) Push frames ('>' prefix): pub/sub messages, client-side caching invalidations, and tracking redirects should switch to push frames when the session's resp_protocol_version == 3. Today we still emit Arrays even after HELLO 3.
(c) Map ('%') replies for HGETALL/CONFIG GET/XINFO STREAM in RESP3 — upstream switches to Map; verify we mirror that.
(d) Set ('~') replies for SMEMBERS/SINTER/SUNION/SDIFF in RESP3.
(e) Double (',') replies for ZSCORE/ZADD INCR in RESP3.
(f) Big number ('(') support for very large counters.
(g) Verbatim string ('=') for INFO/CLUSTER NODES.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend live-oracle harness to negotiate HELLO 3 and re-run the relevant cases, comparing against upstream's RESP3 wire frames.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenredis","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T03:29:37.879651311Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hello","protocol","push","resp3"]}

(a) HELLO 3: server response should include a Map instead of an Array for the metadata; verify client receives '%' framing not '*'.
(b) Push frames ('>' prefix): pub/sub messages, client-side caching invalidations, and tracking redirects should switch to push frames when the session's resp_protocol_version == 3. Today we still emit Arrays even after HELLO 3.
(c) Map ('%') replies for HGETALL/CONFIG GET/XINFO STREAM in RESP3 — upstream switches to Map; verify we mirror that.
(d) Set ('~') replies for SMEMBERS/SINTER/SUNION/SDIFF in RESP3.
(e) Double (',') replies for ZSCORE/ZADD INCR in RESP3.
(f) Big number ('(') support for very large counters.
(g) Verbatim string ('=') for INFO/CLUSTER NODES.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend live-oracle harness to negotiate HELLO 3 and re-run the relevant cases, comparing against upstream's RESP3 wire frames.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenredis","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T03:50:23.174897054Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T03:50:23.173045710Z","close_reason":"Completed in fc3d541","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hello","protocol","push","resp3"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r73v","title":"[HIGH] RDB+AOF persistence round-trip parity: load upstream-produced .rdb + .aof and re-emit byte-identical on save","description":"Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 3 (cross-impl round-trip) to RDB + AOF persistence:

(a) Load a corpus of upstream-produced dump.rdb files covering every type tag we emit (string, list_quicklist_2, set/intset/listpack, hash/listpack, zset_2/listpack, stream_listpacks_3, plus 7.4 hash_metadata family). Verify each loads cleanly and matches the in-memory state upstream would produce. Resave; feed back to upstream redis-server --loadrdb; verify XINFO STREAM FULL / HGETALL / etc. parity.
(b) Same for AOF: load upstream-produced appendonly.aof files (with RDB-preamble + multi-DB SELECT + EXPIREAT + complex transactions), verify replay + resave path is byte-stable.
(c) Re-encode our RDB with --rdbcompression yes (upstream's default) and verify upstream loads the LZF-encoded strings. Today we never emit LZF on save.
(d) Verify the RDB version header (REDIS0011 for 7.2, REDIS0012 for 7.4) matches what we negotiate.

The DUMP/RESTORE round-trip we just landed (br-frankenredis-v8s5) is at the wire level for single keys; this bead is the FILE level for full snapshots.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aof","persistence","rdb","roundtrip"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r74v","title":"[HIGH] Pub/Sub scalability + keyspace notifications: SSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH (sharded), CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS, K/E notify masks","description":"(a) Sharded pub/sub (Redis 7+): SSUBSCRIBE/SUNSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH against shard-channels. CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS / SHARDNUMSUB introspection. Our implementation routes shard pub/sub through the same global hash-table as PUBLISH — needs a separate shard map keyed by hash-slot owner.
(b) Keyspace notifications: CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events 'KEA' should turn on __keyspace@<db>__:<key> and __keyevent@<db>__:<event> message generation for every applicable command. Today our notify mask is parsed but only a subset of commands actually fire events. Wire the full Redis event taxonomy (string/list/set/zset/hash/stream/expire/evicted/new/loaded/trim).
(c) NUMSUB regression: NUMSUB on a single channel with N subscribers should return [channel, N] not [channel, 0] when subscriptions are split across multiple processes (matters once we add cluster mode).
(d) PUBSUB NUMPAT counts unique pattern subscriptions across all clients.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 6: drive scenarios from a multi-client harness (extends fr_p2c_006 replication journey infrastructure).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["keyspace-notifications","pubsub","scalability"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-r75v","title":"[HIGH] Lua scripting parity audit: EVAL/EVALSHA/EVAL_RO + FUNCTION CREATE/LOAD/CALL/FLUSH/DUMP/RESTORE + script-side redis.* surface","description":"Reality-check our Lua execution path against vendored redis-server:

(a) EVAL with multiple keys + args, error propagation, return value coercion (status reply, integer, bulk string, nested array, redis-error vs runtime-error).
(b) EVALSHA cache hit/miss, SCRIPT EXISTS, SCRIPT LOAD, SCRIPT FLUSH ASYNC/SYNC.
(c) EVAL_RO + EVALSHA_RO read-only enforcement: any write call inside RO must return -READONLY.
(d) FUNCTION LOAD with the !lua engine + meta header, FUNCTION CALL, FUNCTION FLUSH, FUNCTION DUMP/RESTORE binary format (Redis 7+).
(e) The redis.* surface inside scripts: redis.call, redis.pcall, redis.error_reply, redis.status_reply, redis.sha1hex, redis.log, redis.replicate_commands(), redis.breakpoint(), redis.debug(), redis.set_repl(), KEYS / ARGV indexing semantics.
(f) MULTI inside EVAL: should be rejected; EXEC inside EVAL should error.
(g) FCALL_RO + script-flag #!lua flags=no-writes / allow-stale / allow-oom / allow-cross-slot-keys.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_scripting.json + core_function.json with each combination, run live-oracle diff.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:05:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["eval","function","lua","scripting"]}
